title: Trelane Message Protocol (TMP) v1.0
description: The fixed set of structured messages exchanged between agents and the Squire. A non-AI consumer decodes any message by reading `type`, then reading exactly the fields documented for that type. Agents read the same fields plus the optional human-readable `body`. The `custom` type is the sole free-text carrier; its content is opaque to the Squire.
$comment: Decode algorithm for a non-AI consumer (the Squire): 1) parse JSON; 2) read the `type` discriminator; 3) dispatch to the handler for that type; 4) read the typed fields listed under that type's variant. Unknown `type` values MUST be rejected, not guessed. The Squire may safely skip any message whose `type` it does not act on (notably `custom`).

tmp_version.description: Protocol version. Consumers reject a message whose version they do not implement rather than partially decoding it.

type.description: The discriminator. Selects which variant's fields are present and how the message is decoded.

from.description: Sender identity: an agent id, or the literal "squire" for Squire-authored messages.

to.description: Recipient agent id. null means the message is not addressed to one agent: a bulletin, a broadcast, or a system-wide notice.

channel.description: direct = addressed to `to` and surfaced in that agent's inbox. bulletin = domain-scoped board, pulled on demand, never wakes anyone.

created_tick.description: The Squire tick at which this message was created. All timeouts and ages in Trelane are counted in ticks, not wall-clock time.

re.description: Id of the message this one replies to, or null. Threads a reply back to what it answers.

supersedes.description: Id of a message this one replaces (e.g. a bulletin update superseding a prior bulletin), or null. Walking the supersedes chain reconstructs how a working set drifted over time.

body.description: Optional human-readable text. Agents read it; the Squire ignores it for every structured type. For the `custom` type it is the entire payload and is REQUIRED.

condition_kind.description: What the parked agent is waiting on. The Squire watches for this condition to become true and wakes the agent when it does.

condition_ref.description: The specific thing being awaited: a message id (reply), a DI request id, a claim path, etc. Must be specific enough that the Squire can detect satisfaction — never a vague state.

parked_since_tick.description: Tick the park was recorded. Feeds the abandon-if-too-old backstop and the starvation guarantee.

wake_kind.description: The single recorded reason this agent is being woken. Exactly one, never zero, never several.

reason_ref.description: The id the wake refers to (the satisfied message, the resolved DI request, the freed claim), or null for reasons that carry no ref (e.g. plain inbox).

domain_change_paths.description: Optional. Paths in the woken agent's own domain that changed since it last saw them — attached so the agent can reconcile per the domain-change rule. Empty or absent means no change detected.

request_id.description: Id of the domain-intrusion request this message opens. Approvals, vetoes, and the eventual resolution all reference it.

path_glob.description: The path or glob the requester wants to write.

purpose.description: REQUIRED and non-empty. Specific statement of what the requester intends to do and why. A vague purpose is grounds for rejection.

objection_deadline_tick.description: Tick after which a standing non-owner approval resolves to Approved if no veto has arrived. Bounds the owner's veto window.

request_id.description: The DI request being approved. Any enabled agent other than the requester may send this.
The DI request being vetoed. Only the registered owner of the target domain may send this.

reason.description: REQUIRED. Why the owner is vetoing. A veto overrides any number of approvals regardless of arrival order.

path.description: The path being leased. A claim reserves the file; it is a separate gate from DI permission. Never valid for .trelane/** or .git/**.

ttl_s.description: Lease duration in seconds. The claim frees automatically when it expires if not renewed.

scope.description: The domain this bulletin is scoped to. Agents interested in this domain pull it; it never wakes anyone.

paths.description: Optional. The files the posting agent is working in or intends to touch. Best-effort; may be absent when only intent is being announced.

then.$comment: channel MUST be "bulletin" for this type. Enforced by the channel enum plus consumer convention.

changed_paths.description: Paths in the recipient's own domain that changed without the recipient's action. The agent reconciles these against history before reacting.

domain.description: The owned domain a Biplane pass has proposed splitting.

proposal_ref.description: Id of the pending reconciliation proposal the owner can review. Informational; does not block the owner's current work.

tick.description: The tick at which total quiescence (zero ready work across every domain at every granularity) was first observed. Informational only — never triggers a wake or an automatic Biplane pass.

body.description: REQUIRED for custom messages. Free text from one agent to another. The Squire never decodes or acts on this content.
